mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate above 5.0% signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer spending capacity, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in greater than 65% of instances when UNRATE exceeds 5.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicate a broad contraction in aggregate demand, forcing equity valuations lower over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint declines in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict negative 20-day returns with statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month drop in the index of industrial production below -0.5% reflects immediate supply chain bottlenecks and manufacturing weakness, triggering short-term negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines of this magnitude are followed by positive 5-day returns in greater than 70% of cases during recovery phases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising inflation combined with a stable or rising federal funds rate indicates real rate tightening, which compresses equity multiples and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising CPIAUCSL and non-falling FEDFUNDS yields a positive 60-day return IC greater than 0.05 over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth directly increases corporate earnings expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show zero or negative correlation with 20-day forward returns across all tested rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in total nonfarm payrolls exceeding 250,000 signals robust labor demand and wage growth, supporting consumer spending and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Payroll additions above 250,000 are followed by negative 20-day returns in greater than 60% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence where industrial production rises while real consumption falls indicates inventory accumulation and weak final demand, causing immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The INDPRO-RSAFS divergence signal fails to predict negative 5-day returns with a Rank IC greater than 0.03 in walk-forward tests.
